pattern: Choosing the neighbor of the first player's node that controls the biggest region wins exactly when one of the three regions around that node exceeds half the tree.
hints: After the first move, the node x splits the rest of the tree into three regions: its left subtree, its right subtree, and everything through its parent.
The second player should claim whichever of those three regions is largest by coloring x's corresponding neighbor.
Find x, measure its left-subtree size and right-subtree size; the parent side is n minus those two minus one. You win if the largest of the three is more than n/2.

edge_inputs.description: second player can win by taking the parent side
x is the root of a tiny tree, no winning region
single node, first player takes everything
x is a leaf so parent side dominates
x at root with balanced halves
x deep on a skewed tree

stress.description: random odd-sized trees with a random labelled x
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(1, min(size, 99)) + 1)
    if n % 2 == 0:
        n += 1
    vals = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    x = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [vals, n, x]
stress.note: n forced odd and values are a permutation of 1..n as the problem requires

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def btreeGameWinningMove(self, root, n, x):
        self.left_size = 0
        self.right_size = 0
        def count(node):
            if not node:
                return 0
            l = count(node.left)
            r = count(node.right)
            if node.val == x:
                self.left_size = l
                self.right_size = r
            return l + r + 1
        count(root)
        parent_side = n - self.left_size - self.right_size - 1
        biggest = max(self.left_size, self.right_size, parent_side)
        return biggest > n - biggest
